title: Nursing Support Worker
how_to_become: Apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website or do a Modern Apprenticeship.
description: Nursing support workers assist registered nurses in delivering a high standard of care and treatment to patients and provide support to patients' families and carers. They work across primary, community, and acute care services in a wide range of settings including hospital wards, GP practices, health centres, and mental health settings.
role_overview: As a nursing support worker, you'll provide a high level of care reflecting what matters to patients as individuals, working in a team with healthcare support workers, nurses, and other healthcare professionals. Good communication skills are essential throughout the patient care pathway.
work_areas: Mental health, learning disabilities, and recovery services

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education is needed.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.modern_apprenticeship: Healthcare Support Modern Apprenticeship with clinical pathway units for nursing. Mandatory units include communication, health and safety, and learning development.
education_and_training.mandatory_induction: Mandatory Induction Standards must be completed within 3 to 6 months of employment.

learning_and_development.note: Career development is planned and reviewed annually through the Personal Development Planning (PDP) cycle.

career_progression.overview: Nursing support workers can progress to senior roles or study to become a registered nurse.
qualifications_for_progression: HNC Healthcare Practice at SCQF level 7
Certificate of Higher Education in Healthcare Practice at SCQF level 7
Open University undergraduate nursing degree (no formal entry qualifications required, must remain employed while studying)

chunks.text: To become a nursing support worker, apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website or do a Modern Apprenticeship in Healthcare Support.

Nursing support workers assist registered nurses in delivering high-standard care and treatment to patients across primary, community, and acute care services in hospital wards, GP practices, health centres, mental health settings, and other environments.

Nursing support workers work in children's services, oncology, cardiac rehabilitation, neurological rehabilitation, orthopaedics, critical care, emergency department, mental health, learning disabilities, paediatric complex care, and dementia care.
A good standard of education is needed. Useful school subjects include Biology, English, and Maths.
Work-based training includes infection control, risk management, patient confidentiality, child protection, and health and safety. Career development is reviewed annually through the PDP cycle.

Nursing support workers can progress to senior nursing support worker or nursing assistant practitioner, or study to become a registered nurse via HNC Healthcare Practice, CertHE in Healthcare Practice, or the Open University undergraduate nursing degree.
